summary: No ship: PR discovery authenticates the writer PR, but auto-merge discards that identity and returns to an ambiguous branch selector. The root-cause fix and both r1 corrections are otherwise structurally coherent.

findings.title: Test-quality: arming drops the authenticated PR identity
findings.body: The executor identifies a trusted PR by number and even documents why disarming must use that number: a branch selector can resolve a fork PR with the same head name. The bump lane nevertheless invokes `auto-merge-or-fail.sh` with `branch`, whose merge/view operations repeat that ambiguous lookup. A same-named fork PR can therefore cause the wrong PR to be armed or make re-arming fail repeatedly, stalling autoDeploy. The crowding witness misses this because its writer PR is already armed, while the arming helper explicitly expects the unsafe branch argument.
findings.recommendation: Carry a stable authenticated selector through mutation: pass `trusted.number` for existing PRs and retain the URL/number returned by `gh pr create` for create/adopt. Add an unarmed-writer-plus-fork witness that requires numeric/URL targeting instead of the branch name.

next_steps: Fix the auto-merge selector and strengthen the corresponding witness before shipping.
Refresh the committed verification evidence after the correction; do not weaken or retarget the existing regression lock.
